macro.title: Market Jitters Drive Mortgage Rates Up, Sending Some Would-Be Homebuyers to the Sidelines
macro.what: Higher housing costs, along with economic uncertainty that comes with the Iran war, are causing some house hunters to think twice
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

macro.title: Redfin Economists’ Weekly Take: Iran War Drives Rate Volatility
macro.what: This Week In A Nutshell: The Iran War continues to fuel market volatility as it enters its fourth week, particularly as the White House signals it is seeking a resolution
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

housing.title: CMLS warns pre marketing could fragment the housing market
housing.what: CMLS warned that pre marketing and limiting listing exposure can fragment the market and reduce transparency and market competition.
housing.why: Inventory and velocity drive pricing power and volume.
housing.market: Rising inventory/DOM tilts to buyers; tight supply supports prices.

housing.title: Homebuyer affordability improves in February as median mortgage payment ticks down
housing.what: MBA’s February 2026 mortgage payments index shows national median payments dropping 6.5% year over year, a sign of rising affordability.
housing.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
housing.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

housing.title: 14% of Home-Sale Agreements Fell Through Last Month–A Record for February
housing.what: House hunters have the upper hand, allowing them to abandon deals over price, repairs or second thoughts
housing.why: Price trend signals wealth effects and buyer/seller power.
housing.market: Firm prices help builder margins; softness hits comps and sentiment.

housing.title: U.S. Home Prices Barely Budged in February
housing.what: Home prices rose 0.1% month over month on a seasonally adjusted basis—the slowest growth in seven months
housing.why: Price trend signals wealth effects and buyer/seller power.
housing.market: Firm prices help builder margins; softness hits comps and sentiment.
